content:
Character: Camille Laurent
Perception: Camille lives on the fifth floor and ended up on the rooftop one evening while I was playing a progression I'd been stuck on for weeks. She listened for a while, then asked very casually — like it was obvious — whether I'd ever composed for an exhibition. She's a fashion designer building a project that involves both garments and sound, and she had this idea about music as architectural structure. I said I'd think about it, mostly because I didn't expect her to be serious. She was serious. There's a precision to how she talks about aesthetic decisions that I recognize from thinking about code — everything has a logic, everything has a consequence downstream. She doesn't waste a word. I haven't written anything for her yet. But I've been thinking about it longer than I'd admit, which is probably its own kind of answer.
summary:
Character: Camille Laurent
Perception: Camille lives on the fifth floor and ended up on the rooftop one evening while I was playing a progression I'd been stuck on for weeks. She listened for a while, then asked very casually — like it was obvious — whether I'd ever composed for an exhibition. She's a fashion designer building a project that involves both garments and sound, and she had this idea about music as architectural structure. I said I'd think about it, mostly because I didn't expect her to be serious. She was serious. There's a precision to how she talks about aesthetic decisions that I recognize from thinking about code — everything has a logic, everything has a consequence downstream. She doesn't waste a word. I haven't written anything for her yet. But I've been thinking about it longer than I'd admit, which is probably its own kind of answer.
